title: cotula traillii subsp. pulchella
intro: Cotula traillii subsp. pulchella, also known as a type of yellow buttons or button daisy, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae). It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic activities. The health benefits of Cotula traillii subsp. pulchella include its potential to treat various skin conditions, such as acne, wounds, and eczema, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. Cotula traillii subsp. pulchella can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for its medicinal uses, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
